body.title: Make LearningPipeline.run_once() atomic: single transaction for process + store
body.result: single-transaction fix: _store_knowledge_node accepts conn kwarg; run_once passes outer conn; mark-processed only commits after all stores succeed; 2 new tests
body.intention: I intend that no learning queue entry is lost due to a partial batch failure.
body.description:
Expert audit (Safety Engineer) found split-transaction bug: LearningPipeline.run_once() marks rows processed=true in an outer transaction, then opens a separate inner transaction to store knowledge nodes. If store fails, rows are already marked consumed — silent data loss. Fix: mark processed only after successful store, or use a single transaction for both operations.

**Affected files:** devices/librarian/learning_pipeline.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; Postgres transactions are the atomicity mechanism
**Scope boundary:** IN — restructure transaction boundary so process+store are atomic; OUT — schema changes, queue design changes
**Completion criteria:** a simulated _store_knowledge_node() failure causes run_once() to NOT mark any rows processed=true; test verifies this
